stories.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
stories.description: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants as war enters fourth week with no end in sight
stories.description: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.

stories.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
stories.description: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: How the Iran war is wasting American resources
As the war in Iran expands, can diplomacy help end it?
stories.description: After weeks of escalation, can diplomacy end the conflict between Iran, the US and Israel? NPR's Adrian Ma poses that question to former Iranian diplomat Hossein Mousavian.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 160 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel 'acted alone' in attack on Iranian gas field
stories.description: Speaking at a news conference, the Israeli leader said Trump had requested that there be no further such attacks.

stories.title: Teenager among first to be executed over Iran's anti-government protests
stories.description: Saleh Mohammadi, a member of Iran's national wrestling team, was found guilty of killing police officers alongside two other men, state media say.

stories.title: Three Palestinian women killed in beauty salon during Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Others were injured when the salon in the southern West Bank was hit, while a Thai worker was killed by falling shrapnel in Israel.

stories.title: 'Very difficult to stop': BBC visits scene of Iran cluster bomb strike on Israel
stories.description: An elderly couple was killed after a bomb flew into their apartment in central Israel and exploded.

stories.title: Why the Strait of Hormuz matters so much in the Iran war
stories.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

stories.title: Iran's intelligence minister Esmail Khatib killed in air strike
stories.description: Israel said it had killed Khatib, whose death was confirmed by Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian on Wednesday.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: Nabatieh under fire: Life inside a war zone in southern Lebanon
stories.description: In the southern Lebanese city of Nabatia, paramedics and residents are living under near-constant air strikes, as Israel targets areas it says are linked to Hezbollah. With much of the population displaced, those who remain face daily danger, limited supplies, and relentless unce...

stories.title: Disarming Hezbollah 'extremely difficult', expert says amid Israel tensions
stories.description: It has been “extremely difficult” for the Lebanese government to disarm Hezbollah since the 2024 ceasefire with Israel, despite ongoing Israeli strikes in Lebanon, said Ziad Majed, professor of Middle East studies at the American University of Paris. He noted that Lebanese author...

stories.title: 'Disaster' for Gulf states if Iran targets desalination plants
stories.description: Iran on Saturday threatened to retaliate by targeting key infrastructure in neighbouring countries if US President Donald Trump follows through on his vow to “obliterate” the Islamic Republic’s power plants amid tensions over a blockage of the Strait of Hormuz. Journalist Hoda Ab...

stories.title: The week in pictures: Strikes on Iran, the Oscars and the UEFA Champions League
stories.description: From continuing US and Israeli strikes on Iran to Nowruz celebrations and a volcanic eruption on the French island of Reunion, FRANCE 24 takes a look at some of the week's most powerful images.

stories.title: Iran threatens key infrastructure in Middle East after Trump ultimatum
stories.description: Iran threatened on Sunday to attack key infrastructure across the Middle East if US President Donald Trump follows through on his vow to "obliterate" the Islamic republic's power plants unless the Strait of Hormuz swiftly reopens. Trump ratcheted up pressure on Iran's leadership,...

stories.title: Displaced in Lebanon sleep on streets as situation turns “catastrophic”
stories.description: Displaced people in Lebanon are increasingly being forced to sleep on the streets as housing becomes unaffordable amid soaring prices, according to Matthieu Karam, editor and photographer at L’Orient-Le Jour. He added that many Lebanese have grown suspicious of those sleeping rou...

stories.title: Iranian missile strikes injure more than 100 in southern Israel
stories.description: Iranian missile strikes on two southern Israeli towns wounded more than 100 people on Saturday, medics said, after Israeli air defence systems failed to intercept the projectiles. The two direct hits tore open the fronts of residential buildings and carved craters into the ground...

stories.title: US claims destroyed Iranian bunker housing weapons
stories.description: The US military declared on Saturday it had taken out an Iranian bunker housing weapons threatening oil and gas shipments in the Strait of Hormuz, as thousands of Iranians marked Eid al-Fitr with prayer. The US statement appeared designed to calm the concerns of energy markets an...

stories.title: Live: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards threaten to completely close Hormuz Strait if US hits energy sites
stories.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards on Sunday threatened to completely close the Strait of Hormuz if US President Donald Trump makes good on his promise to target Iranian energy facilities unless the key transport route is fully reopened within 48 hours. The Revolutionary Guards also sai...

stories.title: Iran strikes Israeli nuclear town in retaliation for Natanz attack amid escalating conflict
stories.description: An Iranian missile struck the Israeli town of Dimona, home to a nuclear facility, injuring dozens in what Tehran said was retaliation for strikes on its Natanz nuclear site. The attack comes amid escalating regional tensions, including two failed Iranian missile strikes on the US...

stories.title: Iranian missile struck town housing nuclear facility: Iran war shows 'no signs of abating'
stories.description: The iran war is showing "no signs of abating" following a missile strike by Tehran on Dimona, a town in southern Israel housing a nuclear facility, FRANCE 24’s Douglas Herbert said in an analysis. There appears to be no "exit tunnel" in sight with Iran seemingly as determined as ...

stories.title: Iran says will hit region’s energy sites if US, Israel target power plants
stories.description: Iran warns of 'irreversibly' destroying vital infrastructure across region after Trump threatens to bomb power plants.

stories.title: Hezbollah attack kills one in north Israel as assault on Lebanon continues
stories.description: Hezbollah has been launching attacks in response to US-Israel war on Iran as more Israeli troops enter southern Lebanon.

stories.title: Did Iran launch missiles at US-UK base on Diego Garcia? Here’s what to know
stories.description: UK condemns what it says is Iranian attack on airbase in Indian Ocean, but Tehran denies it carried out the launches.

stories.title: Moment Iranian strike hits central Tel Aviv
stories.description: Watch the moment an Iranian strike hits what appears to be a bridge in central Tel Aviv, startling pedestrians nearby.

stories.title: ‘Opening Strait of Hormuz will probably require US boots on the ground’
stories.description: ‘Opening Strait of Hormuz will probably require US boots on the ground’

stories.title: Iran war is creating ‘heightened risks of instability across countries in A
stories.description: David Owiro says that African countries are particularly vulnerable to the economic consequences of the Iran war.

stories.title: Aftermath of Iranian missile strikes near Israel’s nuclear facility
stories.description: It was the first time Iranian missiles had penetrated Israeli air defence systems in the area around a key nuclear site.

stories.title: Lebanese nurse refuses to leave her post at hospital despite losses
stories.description: Lebanese nurse remains at her post in the Bekaa Valley after losing her fiance in the 2024 Israel-Hezbollah war.

stories.title: Woman confronts Ben-Gvir at site of Iranian missile strike
stories.description: Israeli woman confronts National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir at the site of an Iranian missile strike in Arad.

stories.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 23 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: Trump threatens to hit Iranian energy sites if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened; Tehran vows to retaliate.

stories.title: Drone footage shows damage in Arad after overnight Iranian missile attack
stories.description: Drone footage shows heavy damage to residential buildings in Arad, Israel, after an overnight Iranian missile strike.

stories.title: China pledges more balanced trade and further opening of the economy after record surplus
stories.description: Chinese Premier Li Qiang pledged on Sunday to further open the country’s economy to foreign firms and pursue more balanced trade with its global partners, after a year marked by trade friction and tariff wars with the United States and European Union in particular. China will imp...

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran with power plant strikes over Hormuz oil blockade
stories.description: US President Donald Trump on Saturday threatened to “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if Tehran does not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours, a significant escalation barely a day after he talked about “winding down” the war. “If Iran doesn’t fully open, without thre...

stories.title: European nations decry ‘increasing settler terror’ in occupied West Bank
stories.description: Diplomats from 13 European countries and Canada on Saturday slammed growing “terror” by settlers against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, after a surge in deadly attacks. Since the start of March, six Palestinians have been shot dead in settler attacks in the West Bank, ac...

stories.title: Kuwait’s civil aviation authority lodges protest against Iranian attacks with ICAO
stories.description: Kuwait’s airspace has been closed since February 28

stories.title: Over 81,000 civilian facilities damaged in Iran since late February — Red Crescent
stories.description: According to his statement cited on the IRCS Telegram channel, among these facilities are medical centers, schools, and ambulance cars

stories.title: Serious damage done to Iran’s power supply facilities in US, Israeli attacks — minister
stories.description: According to the minister, attacks targeted "dozens of water supplies and desalination facilities and destroyed part of the strategic distribution grids"

stories.title: At least 15 wounded in Iran’s missile attacks on Tel Aviv — medics
stories.description: According to it, one man was taken to a hospital in condition of medium gravity, 14 others received minor injuries

stories.title: Killings of Iranian leaders to have serious consequences — Kremlin spokesman
stories.description: The killings of Iranian leaders are the reality that the world lives in, Peskov pointed out, saying he was hopeful that this would not become normality

stories.title: Iranian army delivers drone strike on Israel’s Ben Gurion airport — media
stories.description: The Iranian attack largely involved Arash-2 drones that are an advanced and more destructive version of Kian and Arash-1 unmanned aerial vehicles

stories.title: Iran warns of retaliatory strikes against US infrastructural facilities in Middle East
stories.description: The relevant statement was made by the General Staff of the Iranian armed forces

stories.title: Standoff with Israel enters new phase after attack against Dimona
stories.description: Speaker of the Parliament of Iran Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf also emphasized that the Israeli airspace is not protected

stories.title: US administration starts discussing opportunity of peace talks with Iran — Axios
stories.description: Washington expects that combat operations will last for two - three weeks more

stories.title: US intel agencies see no sign of Iranian government’s 'imminent collapse' — media
stories.description: The media outlet notes that the Washington administration "is still working to assess who is actually in charge in Tehran"

stories.title: Energy supply disruptions caused by Iran conflict may last for years — media
stories.description: According to the news outlet, any swift ceasefire or arrangement allowing safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz won't undo supply shocks

stories.title: US must stop attacks on Iran to restore navigation in Hormuz Strait — top diplomat
stories.description: On March 2, Major General Ebrahim Jabari of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned that the Strait of Hormuz, through which approximately one-fifth of the world's oil exports passes, would be closed to shipping due to Israeli and US military action against Iran

stories.title: Israel strikes Tehran facility allegedly involved in nuclear weapons development — army
stories.description: Iran’s authorities have repeatedly denied allegations that the country has a program to create nuclear weapons

stories.title: Israeli Air Force did not attack Iran’s Natanz nuclear facility — army source
stories.description: Earlier, Iran notified the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) that its Natanz nuclear site had been attacked

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
